title: per-request token (multi-tenancy backbone)
description: Replace the static api_token with a per-request one via a contextvar. cognee_client.py: _get_headers() reads from a contextvar; server.py: middleware in run_http_with_cors extracts Authorization: Bearer -> contextvar. Keep in a separate module/commit for upstream PR-1. SPEC.md sec.6 items 1-2, sec.4.2-4.3.
acceptance_criteria: One API-mode instance serves 2 JWTs -> 2 cognee-API users -> separate datasets.
notes: Implemented in commit 0af1caa. DESIGN DEVIATION from issue text: no ASGI middleware in run_http_with_cors — tool handlers run in the MCP session task, not the ASGI request task, so a middleware-set contextvar would NOT propagate in stateful streamable HTTP. Instead auth_context.get_request_token() reads the Bearer header from request_ctx.get().request (the SDK attaches the starlette Request per message for both streamable-HTTP and SSE; stdio falls back to static --api-token). Contextvar override (set_request_token) kept as the hook for the OAuth layer (.2/.3) to inject an exchanged cognee token. server.py needed NO changes. Verified: 11 new tests incl. full-stack integration (real uvicorn + MCP client, two callers -> two Authorization headers on one client instance). Residual: live E2E against real cognee-API (2 JWTs -> separate datasets) folds into .4 deploy verification.

title: built-in OAuth 2.1 AS (replaces mcp-auth-proxy)
description: Implement OAuthAuthorizationServerProvider (~10 methods) delegating to Google + a Workspace allowlist. Enable via FastMCP(auth_server_provider=..., auth=AuthSettings(client_registration_options=enabled)). Persist clients/codes/refresh (Postgres), not in-memory. Separate module/commit for upstream PR-2. SPEC.md sec.4.4, sec.6 item 3.
design: Provider over Google: GoogleWorkspaceOAuthProvider implements OAuthAuthorizationServerProvider (SDK mcp==1.27); DCR enabled; persistent store via SQLAlchemy async (MCP_OAUTH_DB_URL, sqlite default / Postgres). Glue to .3: load_access_token (runs per HTTP request in ASGI task) calls token_exchanger(email) -> cognee JWT, attaches to CogneeAccessToken.cognee_token; starlette auth middleware puts AuthenticatedUser in ASGI scope; tool handlers read it sync via request_ctx.get().request.scope['user'] (auth_context step added by .3). Callback via mcp.custom_route GET /auth/google/callback.
acceptance_criteria: Claude.ai connects directly (DCR/PKCE); mcp-auth-proxy removed from the pod.
notes: Implemented (merge fba2947): src/oauth_provider.py (GoogleWorkspaceOAuthProvider, DCR, PKCE via SDK, Google delegation, closed-by-default allowlist hd-or-email-domain, single-use codes, refresh rotation, token_exchanger hook), src/oauth_store.py (SQLAlchemy async; sqlite default / Postgres via MCP_OAUTH_DB_URL; sha256-hashed token storage; lazy expiry purge), +21-line server.py wiring behind MCP_OAUTH_ENABLED. Env: GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, MCP_PUBLIC_BASE_URL, GOOGLE_ALLOWED_WORKSPACES/EMAILS, MCP_OAUTH_DB_URL/PATH. 25 tests incl. full flow over HTTP with real S256 PKCE. Gaps: pending authorize txns in-memory (restart mid-login = restart flow); lifetimes not env-tunable; lazy purge only. Residual acceptance (Claude.ai connects, proxy removed from pod) -> .4 deploy.

title: identity bridge (Google user -> cognee auth)
description: Decide option A (per-user api-key) vs B (custom fastapi_users JWT backend in cognee trusting an RS256 JWT with sub=email). B is cleaner but patches cognee. SPEC.md sec.4.6, sec.6 item 4, sec.8 q.1.
design: DECISION (SPEC 8.1): neither pure A nor B. Mint fastapi_users-compatible HS256 JWTs sharing FASTAPI_USERS_JWT_SECRET via pod env (sub=user UUID, aud=fastapi-users:auth). cognee stays vanilla; users auto-provisioned via stock POST /api/v1/auth/register; email->user_id map persisted (SQLAlchemy, sqlite default / Postgres DSN). Verified against cognee 1.1.0 sources in venv: Bearer backend is plain fastapi_users JWTStrategy with secret from FASTAPI_USERS_JWT_SECRET.

title: multi-tenant fork + built-in OAuth 2.1
description: One MCP instance -> N users (own graph per user) + the server itself as an OAuth 2.1 AS for Claude.ai, without mcp-auth-proxy and without a wrapper. Result: one container instead of three. See SPEC.md.
design: mcp==1.27.0 (native OAuth: auth_server_provider/AuthSettings/DCR). Multi-user backbone exists (API mode). Blocker: static api_token (server.py:1942) -> per-request via contextvar. cognee-API trusts only real JWT/api-key.

title: single-container deploy + upstream PR strategy
description: Build the fork image, update the pod kube manifest (drop mcp-auth-proxy, repoint the reverse proxy to the MCP port). E2E with two accounts -> separate graphs. Upstream: PR-1 (per-request), after merge PR-2 (OAuth). SPEC.md sec.7, sec.9.
